steps.title: Identify the required similarity
steps.content: The student's goal is to emphasize a similarity between two specific sculptures: Seated Woman and Woman Fixing Her Hair. To accomplish that goal, the sentence must mention both works and identify something they share. A sentence about only one sculpture, the artist's biography, or Caldwell's overall career would not compare the two sculptures.

steps.title: Check each option against the goal
steps.content:
• Choice A discusses only Seated Woman, so it cannot establish a similarity between two sculptures.
  • Choice B describes Caldwell as a creator of prints and sculptures, but it does not address the two named works.
  • Choice D gives Caldwell's birth year, which is a biographical detail rather than a comparison of the sculptures.

steps.title: Name the shared material
steps.content: Choice C names both Seated Woman and Woman Fixing Her Hair and states that both are made of mahogany. That directly emphasizes the similarity the student wants to highlight. The answer is C.
